summary: `examples/lead-app-ts` is the dogfood SaaS surface for this slice. A small store module was enough, but benchmark prep had an implicit assumption that only `main.ts` and `lead.ts` ever needed copying, which broke once persistence support added a helper module.
ergonomics: The existing lead app routes were already isolated enough that swapping storage behind them stayed local.
Bun-based app tests are convenient for the route flow, but Bun cannot import `node:sqlite`, so the store needed a runtime adapter instead of a straightforward Node-only import.
Full verification is valuable here because it caught the benchmark prep assumption immediately.
follow_ups: Add an explicit app-level SQLite verify script or README note for the persisted database path so the persistence surface is easier to discover outside tests.
If more SQLite-backed app work is coming, centralize the Bun/Node SQLite adapter instead of keeping it local to the lead app.
Add real schema migration steps once the lead app schema changes beyond version 1 instead of only compatibility rejection.
warnings: `benchmarks/test-task-prep.sh` mirrors example files into older task workspaces; if you add new support files under `examples/lead-app-ts/src/server`, update that copy list or verification will fail silently later in the pipeline.
Do not import `node:sqlite` directly in Bun-tested app code. Bun test rejects it even though Node 22 supports it.
`createServer` now owns the SQLite handle lifecycle; callers need to stop the server to release the DB cleanly.
tests_run: nix develop -c bash -lc 'cd examples/lead-app-ts && npm test'
residual_risks: The app now depends on SQLite support from the active runtime; the store bridges Bun via `bun:sqlite` and Node via `node:sqlite`, so future runtime API drift in either experimental surface would need adapter updates.
Schema handling is intentionally narrow: only version `1` is accepted and there is no forward migration path yet beyond rejecting incompatible on-disk databases.
